description: Fix bugs in the MCP server: misleading timeout error message, network capture state tracking race, redundant disableNetworkCapture dispatch in analyze-site, and detectPrimaryApiBaseUrl depth bias.

userStories.title: Fix misleading timeout error message in handleToolProgress
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want accurate timeout duration in error messages so that I can determine the correct retry strategy.
acceptanceCriteria: When the progress-reset timer near the MAX_DISPATCH_TIMEOUT_MS ceiling fires, the error message reports the actual timeout duration (nextTimeout) instead of always reporting DISPATCH_TIMEOUT_MS
The error message distinguishes between a per-progress timeout and the absolute ceiling timeout
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts, line 524.

When a tool reports progress near the 5-minute absolute ceiling (MAX_DISPATCH_TIMEOUT_MS = 300000), the new timer duration is `Math.min(DISPATCH_TIMEOUT_MS, remainingMax)` (line 520). If `remainingMax` is 10 seconds, the timer fires after 10s. But the rejection message on line 524 always says `timed out after ${DISPATCH_TIMEOUT_MS}ms` (30000ms), regardless of the actual timeout.

Fix: Change line 524 from:
```typescript
pending.reject(new Error(`Dispatch ${pending.label} timed out after ${DISPATCH_TIMEOUT_MS}ms`));
```
to:
```typescript
pending.reject(new Error(`Dispatch ${pending.label} timed out after ${nextTimeout}ms`));
```

The `nextTimeout` variable is already in scope (line 520). This requires capturing `nextTimeout` in the setTimeout closure, which it already is since it's in the enclosing function scope.

Note: the immediate rejection at lines 514-516 when `remainingMax <= 0` already correctly uses `MAX_DISPATCH_TIMEOUT_MS` in its message — leave that one alone.

userStories.title: Fix network capture state tracking on dispatch timeout
userStories.description: As a user of browser_enable_network_capture, I want the server's activeNetworkCaptures set to stay in sync with the extension's actual capture state so that export-har works correctly even after a timeout.
acceptanceCriteria: state.activeNetworkCaptures.add(tabId) runs regardless of whether dispatchToExtension succeeds or fails (i.e., it is moved before the dispatch or into a finally block)
If the dispatch fails, the tabId is still added to activeNetworkCaptures so subsequent tools can attempt to interact with it
A comment explains that the extension may have started capture before the timeout, so we track optimistically

userStories.title: Fix double disableNetworkCapture dispatch in analyze-site
userStories.description: As an operator of analyze-site, I want network capture cleanup to happen only once so that the analysis completes faster and without noisy error logs.
acceptanceCriteria: When getNetworkRequests fails in analyze-site's Step 6, disableNetworkCapture is dispatched exactly once (not twice)
The finally block at lines 1032-1039 still runs disableNetworkCapture for the normal (non-error) path
A boolean flag tracks whether capture was already disabled in the catch block

userStories.title: Fix detectPrimaryApiBaseUrl depth bias over coverage
userStories.description: As a plugin developer using analyze-site, I want the primary API base URL to reflect the broadest common prefix so that I get the actual API base URL rather than a narrow sub-path.
acceptanceCriteria: detectPrimaryApiBaseUrl prefers prefixes with higher endpoint count (coverage) over deeper paths that match fewer endpoints

Given endpoints /api/v1/users, /api/v1/posts, /api/v1/comments, /api/v1/auth/login, /api/v1/auth/logout, the function returns https://example.com/api/v1 (covers 5) not https://example.com/api/v1/auth (covers 2)
The existing unit tests still pass
